title: Climate Backsliding Without Constitutionally Sufficient Replacement
description: The executive branch rolls back major climate protections without simultaneously replacing them with measures constitutionally sufficient in aggregate. Courts declare a violation under Art XVIII §1.2, order a remedial plan, and retain jurisdiction. The political branches adopt a sufficient plan. Tests the anti-backsliding rule and the boundary between judicial declaration and judicial prescription of policy instruments.

details.action: administrative rollback of major greenhouse-gas regulations without replacement measures
